entry.title: Missing SAP Service and Asset Manager files to download in SAP for Me
entry.content:
Hi! 

I need to download the SAP Service and Asset Manager files and MDK to debug some issues. But i couldn't find the documments in Download center. 

I follow this steps, but couldn't find.

Search By Category → SAP Mobile Solutions → SAP SERVICE AND ASSET MANAGER → MDK PLUG-IN SDK.

 

Best Regards, 

Rafael Eij Umeda

SAP Service and Asset Manager

entry.title: Help Global System Settings FIN-CS
entry.content:
Hello Experts!

The options:

  • Reclassification: Selection Object in Trigger

  • Validation in SAP S/4HANA

  • Breakdown Category: Selection Object in Maximum Selection

  • Currency Translation: Selection Object

  • Use Item Role Attribute

do not appear in the Global System Settings (Transaction CXB3), and SAP is asking me to change them before upgrading to S/4HANA 2023.

However, I don't understand how to do this, because in Note 3326805, it says to activate these fields, but they do not appear in this transaction (CXB3) . I would like to understand the relevance of this, whether I really need to change it, and how to do so.

Thank you very much!

Attached is how CXB3 appears for me.

entry.title: Restrict the restricted user status batches for specific storage type
entry.content:
Hello Experts!

Is there any way to allow only unrestricted use batches in specific storage type and restrict the restricted use status batches in EWM.

SAP Supply Chain Management EWM - Goods Movement MAN Batch Management 

Thank you,

Vinod

entry.content:
Hi,

Thank you for sharing this blog.

I would like to know how you created the channel for Asset Performance Management in Step 4.

Regards,
Shirsendu Roy

entry.title: 🌟 Community Spotlight: Our Top Contributors May 2025 🌟
entry.content:
Today, we are launching a new blog post series to celebrate the SAP Community’s top contributors.
Our engaged SAP Community members who share their expertise, write valuable content, and help others by answering questions.

Your contributions matter and you help shape the community spirit. We appreciate every one of you, and this blog post is for you - to say thank you! 🎉

The new platform introduced new leaderboards that we want to get better at recognising and celebrating.
If you are not aware of our new leaderboards, you will find links to them in the list below. You will also find the leaderboards in the sidebar widgets throughout the Community. The leaderboards adapt contextually to the area of the community you are in, and the leaderboard on the Community homepage is for the community overall.

Top Accepted Solution Authors:

Our community members who took the time to help others by answering questions, and who had the most answers accepted as solutions within their area of expertise. 

  • CRM and Customer Experience: @WillYoung
  • Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP): @Edrilan_Berisha
  • Financial Management: @Jeremy_Deo
  • Human Capital Management (HCM): @PL1
  • Product Lifecycle Management (PLM): @DonWilliams
  • Spend Management: @Prem_Tracz
  • Supply Chain Management (SCM): @DominikTylczyn
  • Technology: @paula_augedahl


Top Kudoed Authors:

Our blog post authors who published valuable content that other community members kudoed. 

  • SAP Community overall: @josh_bentley
  • CRM and Customer Experience: @EmmanuelDesert
  • Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP): @Caetano
  • Financial Management: @SiljaHuebner
  • Human Capital Management (HCM): @SF-Dan
  • Product Lifecycle Management (PLM): @laura_leote
  • Spend Management: @cherry_chen2
  • Supply Chain Management (SCM): @ManoelCosta
  • Technology: @NachshonVagmayster


This first version of the blog post series focuses on the Community overall leaderboard and the “Products and Technology” categories leaderboards. We are working on bringing the recognition to our Interest Groups.
We also know that some of you participate here in other ways, such as kudoing content, and we aim to recognise that too.


Want to be in next month’s spotlight?

Keep doing what you’re doing: write quality blog posts that other community members kudo and share your knowledge by answering questions. Help each other. Engage with other community members in the comments and in our group discussions.

A shout-out to our community members who ask questions:

If you have asked a question, please remember to accept correct answers as solutions.

When you receive a helpful response, marking it as the Accepted Solution allows others with similar issues to find the best answer quickly. This feature also highlights the most useful responses for future reference.
By accepting answers as solutions, you also give your fellow community members who take the time to help you, their deserved recognition on our leaderboards. 

 

Note: If you're checking the kudo leaderboards and notice the numbers are off—no worries! We're already aware of the issue and are actively working on fixing it.

entry.title: Re: Reusable Logger Class in ABAP – Simple Logging in Reports
entry.content: In the Part 1 blog post mentioned by Raymond, also read the comments which refer to other blog posts or github repositories presenting other custom log classes.

entry.content: Issue got resolved after maintaining logical port.

entry.title: Re: How to maintain the SOBES field for specific combinat...
entry.content: Transaction OMD9 (SPRO Production, Material Requirements Planning, Master Data, Define Special Procurement Type

entry.title: Domain Certificate Renewal *businessbydesign.cloud.sap and *sapbydesign.com
entry.content:
The existing server certificate for domain *businessbydesign.cloud.sap and *sapbydesign.com will be renewed as it is going to expire on DD/MM/YYY.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Domain Certificate Renewal *businessbydesign.cloud.sa...
entry.content: In order to renew the certificate, refer to the KBA: https://me.sap.com/notes/0003512176

entry.title: Re: Error "???COMMON_Survey_Invitation_Content???"
entry.content: Thank you so much for the additional information on this error! While there is no fix yet, it is promising that the SAP team of experts is working on a fix.

entry.title: Suggestion on relevant Certification!
entry.content:
Hello Experts!

I am an SAP Project Manager with 20+ years, started as an ABAP consultant! I have managed projects in ECC, S/4 HANA Migration, SAP Agile Delivery, Succesfactors and Payroll...and so on. I am currently pursuing my PgMP and would like to take up a acertification in SAP AI Project management to be future ready. Please suggest me a good one!

Kind regards

Archana

entry.title: Re: Sharing Power, Doubling Success - That's Co-Leadership @ SAP! 🚀 #3 Tandem
entry.content: Is there a statement of SAP, on how this concept of Co-Leadership / Dual Leadership can be implemented in SAP SuccessFactors? To my knowledge it is not supported here, mainly as "multipositioning" is not allowed there. Glad to hear otherwise.

entry.title: Re: Poland VAT exchange rate in Account Payables process...
entry.content: Thank for your proposal but this set up is already in place (manual f/x entry). So unfortunately this is not the solution.

entry.title: Re: Reusable Logger Class in ABAP – Simple Logging in Reports
entry.content: Here is a similar logger of mine, built on top of SLG0: Simbal

entry.title: Re: Table FQM_FLOW not updated
entry.content: Well the problem in our case it, that this does not happen for line items. All line items need invoice receipt or EKPO-EREKZ active to be deleted. We upgraded last week to Release S/4HANA 2023. Before our upgrade we dont had this issue.

entry.title: Need to run system status report multiple times in order to use the support user.
entry.content:
Hi All,
We have a few companies are using the Support user in all of them. Initially, we had to run the System Status Report in RSP before we could log into the companies. RSP was running weekly, but still had to run the SSR for each company individually and upload it before we could log in the companies.

The SSR in RSP runs weekly, last one ran on 04/06/2025. Today when I had to log into the various companies, I had to run the SSR again before I could log into each company. 
Is it suppose to work like this. Had anyone experienced this as well.

We are on version 10.0 FP2208
RSP: 3.2 PL19

Thank you all,
Jerusha

entry.title: Re: Poland VAT exchange rate in Account Payables process...
entry.content:
IMHO, if there is a standard solution for your version, it should be referenced in the KBA. Unfortunately, you will have to consult your Abapers. For example, you could use a substitution rule with a user exit.

You could also

  • Raise an issue  with the support
  • Use Customer Influence.

entry.title: Supplier direct transfer approach - BUT000 no entry
entry.content:
Hello dear SAP Community,

currently, I am trying to migrate suppliers from an SAP ERP system to a HANA 2022 SP4 system using the direct transfer approach in the Migration Cockpit. Unfortunately, I am facing two issues:

  1. Business partners are not being migrated, only suppliers (no entry in BUT000).
    1. The structure: with the BP ROLES is filled with 2 entries.
  2. The internal number assignment is not working. I receive the message:
    1. Business partner ##1 is not present --- R1-201.

By using the Staging approach with XML the function module is filled nearly the same and everything is working fine.

Thank you for your advice and best regards,

Lukas

entry.title: Re: HU Label not printed for HUs loaded as initial stock...
entry.content:
Hi Amod_V

According to the help, the HU Label output by the following processes:

"The HU label is relevant for all HU types and packaging material types.

The HU label is issued at the time of HU creation in the following apps:

  • Change Inbound Deliveries - Deliveries

  • Change Inbound Deliveries - Production

  • Pack Outbound Deliveries

  • Pack Warehouse Stock

The supported channel is printout."

So the implication is that the migration cockpit might not trigger the output.

As you have the HUs created and available in the WM Monitor, you might need to look at a manual process to print the labels.

In WM Monitor, you can output the list of HUs and even export it to Excel

The label itself is pretty simple - it is basically the barcode and the Pack. Mat. type

You don't mention which label printer model you have but most have some basic label printing software and most of those allow you to do some sort of mass print of labels. 

With the generated Excel or .csv you should be able to print them in mass pretty quickly. At least you should be able test it out on a batch of 25 or 50 to determine feasibility pretty quick.

PS, the help also has this recommendation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/87f9b54f9c4f4e75aff0061860a6589a/571fc3d95d564f55b9ed18f581bf3567.html?locale=en-US

I hope this helps - let us know how it turns out

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: Inconsistencies Between Quality and Production Environments in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content: Hi, depending on the configuration for these functionalities, you could try and compare the configurations as Venu also said. More info on this can be found her: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b249d650b15e4b3d9fc2077ee921abd0/826e3120d0cd43d4a5cf75a93e07037d.html. Hope this helps. If you feel you have validated the config and transports and confirmed everything looks similar and good I suggest you create a support case/ticket. This way Product Support can help analyze. Good luck! Esther

If the proxy is consumer service, you can try used this configuration the service:

entry.title: Re: Missing SAP Service and Asset Manager files to downlo...
entry.content: That is the correct path.  I suspect your use account does not have the necessary license/permissions attached to be able to see and download the files you are seeking.  I would suggest checking with your Account team further.

entry.title: Re: Exciting Update: SAP SuccessFactors Customer Training Courses are Moving to learning.sap.com!

HI!  Update - The 1H 2025 Project Team Orientation (PTO) and Administration (Admin) training courses are live now in SAP Learning.  This guide includes step-by-step instructions: SAP SuccessFactors Customer Training & SFX Quick Start Guide. Access training courses with direct links here: SAP SuccessFactors HCM Training Materials Guide.

Tamara

entry.title: Re: ADT: Edit Window titles
entry.content:
I created an eclipse plugin to do this for now, since the functionality is not available as Standard. I used the Project title from eclipse project, to replace the default System ID on Page title.

Final result looks something like this:

Link to plugin: https://github.com/tjuwin1/PublicFiles/blob/ff44c77d40aec06c33128bc4def56e2468f10847/abapTabTitles_1.0.0.202506061032.jar

entry.title: Re: Table FQM_FLOW not updated
entry.content: Please check if the note 3501295 - Flow builder Plus: PO items are not getting deleted after deletion, can solve your issue. If not, you can create SAP ticket.

entry.title: Re: Query Regarding Cost Visibility in Actual Cost Report...
entry.content:
Hello Bharat23,

let me try to answer your questions: 

1. Should GRN Costs for Purchased Components Appear in the Actual Cost Report for the Revenue Project?

Yes, they should be reflected.

In SAP Public Cloud (and ECC), when you do a Goods Receipt (GR) for a purchase order (PO) referencing a production order assigned to a WBS element or revenue project, the actual costs are:

  • Captured at the WBS element level via account assignment category Q (project stock).

  • Tracked in the project actual cost reports such as:

    • Project Cost Report (F0719)

    • Plan/Actual Overview App (F0718)

    • Project Actuals (F0714)

This happens because the GR for a PO with a project assignment updates the actuals in the WBS/Project.

2. Which GL Account is Hit: Cost Allocation or Budget Planning GL?

The GL account that is hit during GR depends on:

  • Valuation class of the material

  • Account determination settings in the OBYC-like logic (automatic account determination) for Public Cloud

  • Account assignment category Q (project stock)

Important: The cost is posted to a primary cost element, which is derived from the valuation class in the material master and the configuration in the SSCUI for automatic postings.

So:

  • It does NOT go to the GL used for budgeting per se.

  • It goes to a cost GL (primary cost element)—aligned to the nature of the cost (e.g., raw material purchase).

  • Budget check happens independently through the Availability Control (AVC) if budget is maintained for the project.

You can check actual postings via:

  • App: Display Line Items in General Ledger (F2217)

  • App: Project Actuals (F0714)

3. What Happens in the Report After Order Closure, Confirmation, and Settlement?

Upon these steps, the following impacts occur:

Order Confirmation

  • Activity confirmation posts actual costs (e.g., labor, machine time) to the production order.

  • If linked to a project, they are later settled to the project during settlement.

Goods Receipt for Header Material

  • The value of the finished product (from BOM + activity cost) is posted.

  • It is moved into Q-stock tied to the project/WBS element.

Settlement of Production Order

  • Costs collected on the production order (materials, activities) are settled to the WBS element (revenue project).

  • This is a primary cost transfer, and will appear as actual costs on the revenue project.

  • You can trace this via the App: Project Actuals (F0714) or settlement log.

Closure

  • Once technically and financially closed, the order stops accepting postings.

  • All actuals are now visible in project reporting only, not the order.

 

Hope that will help.

 Best regards,

Patryk Waledziak

entry.title: CJIA report payment issue
entry.content:
1) Payment schedule .

  1. First step - A purchase order was created, with an account assignment category for the project (WBS element) and mandatory down payments on the items of the purchase order.

The initial data is as follows:

Purchase cost – 90,000.00

Expected delivery date 01.10.2025

Expected payment date of the Advance payment 60% - 01.06.2025

Expected post-payment date in 90 days from the delivery date – 30.12.2025

Accordingly, the expected financing (liability) for the order for 90,000.00 by period looks as follows:

01.06.2025 – 54,000.00

30.12.2025 – 36,000.00

In the CJIA report, the financial liability (type 51) for the above order is displayed in the 1st line with the expected payment date as 30.12.2025 – 90,000.00, which does not comply with the terms of the contract and does not take into account the need to pay an advance of 54,000.00 on 01.06.2025, as well as the payment amount on 31.12.2025 is incorrectly formed (correctly 36,000.00).

How to ensure the reflection of the expected financing in terms of periods, taking into account advance payments?

2) We have activated the Project Cash Management feature.

  1. First step - A purchase order was created - with an account assignment category for the project (WBS element) and mandatory down payments on the items of the purchase order.

 

The total purchase order value is €225,000. The amount of down payments to be paid to the supplier for this purchase order is €135,000.

  1. At this point, we have the following values in the CJIA report (Project Actual and Commt Paymt LIs) - there is a total order amount with a value type of 51 “ Purchase order (PO)”. But there is no information at all about the amount of down payments to be paid to the supplier.

  1. Next step - A Down Payment Request was created via ME2DP “Down-Payment Monitoring for PO”.

  1. At this point, the report CJIA (Project Actual and Commt Paymt LIs) displays the amount of the Down payment request with the value type 58 “ Down payment request”. But there is no reduction of the value 51 “ Purchase order (PO)”. And the total amount of expected payments in Project management is incorrect.

Questions:

Why doesn't the CJIA report show the expected down payments that have been completed on purchase order line items?

Why doesn't the Down payment request created from a purchase order reduce the expected payments for that purchase order?

entry.title: Setting up Custom email templates for SuccessFactors Employee Central Time Sheet
entry.content:
I would like to check if we can set up custom email templates for SuccessFactors Employee Central Time Sheet.

We have different scenarios where we would like to set up custom Email templates but we could not find any tokens that can be used to refer EC Time Sheet data. Like Time Sheet Start Date, Time sheet End Date, Status of the Time Sheet, Time Sheet Duration etc.

Has some one prepared custom email templates with above Time Sheet data included in the template ?

entry.title: Re: Simplify SSO with Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) & SAP Identity Authentication Service
entry.content: Hi, is there a guide for SAP IAS as the IdP to Entra?

entry.title: Re: Use Change Audit Reports
entry.content:
Hi

I've been trying to generate an audit report in Change Audit Report/Create Configuration Data Report/Proxy Assignment Change - I've tried making a change on an employee level [adding them to a group which has proxy rights], that didn't work.  I made a global change in Company system and logo settings by unchecking the 'Disable proxy access for users without proxy rights' but neither of those changes prompted a mention in the report.  Would you know how/where I can generate a change that will display in the report?

Thanks

Páidí Campbell

entry.title: Re: Exciting Update: SAP SuccessFactors Customer Training Courses are Moving to learning.sap.com!
entry.content:

In addition, the SFALC allowed downloading the PTO. Is this still possible in the learning.sap.com portal?

entry.title: Re: Need to find BOM chang history CDS View for changes done & Mail or notification to respectiv

entry.title: After S4 upgrade ,style.css files are not loading for SAP ui5 apps in flp
entry.content:
Hi,

We have recently upgraded from S4 2018 to 2023, After S4 upgrade ,style.css files are not loading for SAP ui5 apps in flp, 

it is working fine in 2018, not in 2023
I have made the below changes in Manifest.json and component .js, but no luck

Kindly help,

 

Best Regards,
Supreeth

entry.title: Re: Suggestion on relevant Certification!
entry.content:
Hi @A-ArchanaNair 

As a seasoned SAP Project Manager with over two decades of experience—including ECC, S/4HANA migrations, SAP Agile delivery, and HR solutions—it’s great that you’re looking into AI to stay ahead of the curve.

You may find the following links helpful for exploring relevant certifications and learning paths:

  1. SAP Certified Associate – SAP Business Data Cloud
    This certification focuses on managing and utilizing data in SAP’s cloud ecosystem—essential knowledge as AI solutions rely heavily on trusted, well-structured data.

  2. SAP Certified Associate – Positioning SAP Business Suite
    A strategic certification that helps you understand how SAP’s legacy suite transitions toward intelligent, AI-augmented platforms.

  3. Provisioning and Implementing Joule – Learning Journey
    This hands-on learning journey walks you through how to enable and manage Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot—great for understanding AI adoption from a project manager’s perspective.

I’m still learning myself, but I hope you find these helpful as you explore SAP AI and certification options. Happy to hear your thoughts or if you’ve come across other useful resources!

entry.title: Help whit a MM02
entry.content:
I need your help in the transacction MM02. 

I don´t how change the fixes location, i try change but i can´t  because showme a error the type of wharehouse is don´t updated in this material-

 

Help me please.

entry.title: Re: After S4 upgrade ,style.css files are not loading for SAP ui5 apps in flp
entry.content: are those files inside your app or inside a theme? Have you rebuild the themes after the upgrade in /n/UI5/THEME_DESIGNER?

entry.title: Why QUnit and sinon versions included in SAPUI5 are so old?
entry.content:
I am developing a SAPUI5 app using version 1.120.31 but I I know this question applies for older versions as well.

I have used the single "freestyle" official template available in BAS. 

Like usual, it adds the test folder with unit and integration tests templates. Let me focus on unit tests here.

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
	<meta charset="utf-8">
	<title>Unit tests for my app</title>
	<script
		id="sap-ui-bootstrap"
		src="../../resources/sap-ui-core.js"
		data-sap-ui-resourceroots='{
			"my/app/name": "../../",
			"unit": "."
		}'
		data-sap-ui-async="true"
		data-sap-ui-compat-version="edge">
	</script>
	<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="../../resources/sap/ui/thirdparty/qunit-2.css">
	<script src="../../resources/sap/ui/thirdparty/qunit-2.js"></script>
	<script src="../../resources/sap/ui/qunit/qunit-junit.js"></script>
	<script src="../../resources/sap/ui/qunit/qunit-coverage.js"></script>
	<script src="../../resources/sap/ui/thirdparty/sinon.js"></script>
	<script src="../../resources/sap/ui/thirdparty/sinon-qunit.js"></script>
	<script src="unitTests.qunit.js"></script>
</head>
<body>
	<div id="qunit"></div>
	<div id="qunit-fixture"></div>
</body>
</html>

Checking the version of such third party libraries, it's possible to see that they are very old. At the time of this question:

qunit is in version 2.24.1 

sinon is in version 20.0.0

To make it simpler to show that SAPUI5 includes old version of such, you can check such links and the header document available inside each of them

https://ui5.sap.com/1.120.31/resources/sap/ui/thirdparty/qunit-2.js

/*!
 * QUnit 2.3.2
 * https://qunitjs.com/
 *
 * Copyright jQuery Foundation and other contributors
 * Released under the MIT license
 * https://jquery.org/license
 *
 * Date: 2017-04-18T02:19Z
 */

https://ui5.sap.com/1.120.31/resources/sap/ui/qunit/qunit-junit.js

/*!
 * OpenUI5
 * (c) Copyright 2009-2025 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company.
 * Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 - see LICENSE.txt.
 */

https://ui5.sap.com/1.120.31/resources/sap/ui/qunit/qunit-coverage.js

/*!
 * OpenUI5
 * (c) Copyright 2009-2025 SAP SE or an SAP affiliate company.
 * Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 - see LICENSE.txt.
 */

https://ui5.sap.com/1.120.31/resources/sap/ui/thirdparty/sinon.js

/**
 * Sinon.JS 1.14.1, 2015/03/16
 *

https://ui5.sap.com/1.120.31/resources/sap/ui/thirdparty/sinon-qunit.js

/**
 * sinon-qunit 2.0.0, 2014/05/29
 *

As you can see, those versions are over 10 years deprecated - why isn't SAP updating these dependencies?

This makes is very difficult to write tests are there are many features availble on those librarys which did not exist back in time - apart from the any other potential risks (security, performance, compatibility with modern browsers, etc) on such dependencies as well I suppose

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content: Hello everyone! Great to be here!  I've been working in SAP since 2000.  I started as an ABAP Developer in a Customer Support group.  At present, I'm part of my organization's S/4HANA migration journey.

entry.title: Re: Exciting Update: SAP SuccessFactors Customer Training Courses are Moving to learning.sap.com!
entry.content:
@romanweise - Hi!  I am happy to clarify.
1. Translations—As part of our usual process, after all the updated courses are live, we request translations from the translation team. The process can take a few weeks, but when the translations are complete, language links will be added to the PTO/Admin standalone courses, and when a language link is selected, the course will be shown in the chosen language.  

2. Downloading courses—The embedded, downloadable course PDF is not a supported feature on learning.sap.com at the moment. It has been requested as a future roadmap consideration.

Thanks!
Tamara

entry.title: Re: Exciting Update: SAP SuccessFactors Customer Training Courses are Moving to learning.sap.com!
entry.content:
@TamaraBauler, thank you very much for your quick reply.

Just to be sure. The non-English language versions will remain available in the SFALC till the translations have been made available in the new learning.sap.com portal?

entry.title: Mastering Date-Based Aggregation in SAP: Assets, Headcount and out of the box YTD with LAST
entry.content:
This is Filippo Naggi, EPM Enterprise Architect with strong SAP Analytics Cloud and OneStream expertise.

In this blog, we will walk through how to set up the accounts that should be aggregated by date, for example, Asset accounts or Headcount .

To begin with, SAC can prioritize aggregation either to the Measure or to the Account dimension.
In this case, we will need to prioritize the Account dimension because we want to set up the type of Exception Aggregation according to the Account.

After this, we can use the Account dimension.

This setup must be done :
Exception Aggregation Type: LAST
Exception Aggregation Dimensions: DATE

Finally, we can open our report and verify that the nre Exception Aggregation is working: Account values are no longer summed up at the Year level, instead the last Month value is used for Aggregation:

Bonus
How to control calculation priorities across different Measures and Accounts ?

In this case, we can use the Custom Priority for calculations:

Once this is enabled, a beautiful interface to assign custom priority to Measures and Accounts will be available in the Model Calculations workspace:

Hope you enjoyed this blog.

I love playing with standard SAC Planning and OneStream functionalities to limit the customization when possible. And this case is a beautiful example of this approach.

Do not hesitate to post any questions here or contact me at filippo.naggi@gmail.com.

Filippo

entry.title: Re: sap public cloud BADI needed for VF02 release to accounting
entry.content: removing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Research and Development as not relevant

entry.title: Turn off e-mail notification for specific users
entry.content: Hello Community!  Do you know if there is a way to turn off e-mail notifications for specific users?

entry.title: Re: After S4 upgrade ,style.css files are not loading for SAP ui5 apps in flp
entry.content: Thank you for your response, those files are inside app

entry.title: Re: After S4 upgrade ,style.css files are not loading for SAP ui5 apps in flp
entry.content: those files are inside the app

entry.title: Notification with Outstanding Task and not there in QMSM table
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are unable to close the notification and it does not appear in QMSM table. In QMEL table we could see that MAKNZ is blank. Is this due to data discrepancy. 

Can we do the configuration to activate tab 10\TAB11 for notification type in (SPRO - Set Screen Templates for Notification Type) and delete the items in Notification and revert the changes back.

Or can we follow SAP Note 1747597 and do the configuration and delete the items in Notification and revert the changes back.

Regards,

Jaya

entry.title: Re: ILX - entity images -> not logical mapping ?
entry.content: Hi CR, regarding Kris' second question, we are having issue with one image for Programs that did not get refreshed for all Programs after 12 hours and while reuploading the defaut and a custom image again, all Programs are now not displaying the uploaded image in the Search Results Page view only. Did you have that issue with Programs? Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Why QUnit and sinon versions included in SAPUI5 are s...
entry.content:
Hey there,

SAP is aware of that too!

Here are some noteworthy PRs to have a look at:

- https://github.com/ui5-community/generator-ui5-ts-app/issues/34 and  

- https://github.com/ui5-community/generator-ui5-ts-app/pull/46 

And finally something from these PRs that might help you, to quote Andreas Kunz: "For external consumption, the go-to way is drafted in https://github.com/SAP/openui5-sample-app/pull/206/files: adding sinon as external dependency and using it via ui5-tooling-modules."

Hope that helps. 🙂

entry.title: Instance creation failed - abap trial
entry.content:
Hello,

I am unable to create instance for abap trial

email id - grvsetu3@gmail.com

@SAPSupport can you fix this. why these errors are frequent? 10 minutes of tutorials taking days and weeks

entry.title: Re: After S4 upgrade ,style.css files are not loading for...
entry.content:
So this likely has to do with the new SAPUI5 version in S/4HANA 2023 (1.120.x)

I recommend changing it to:

        "resources": {
            "css": [
                {
                    "uri": "css/style.css"
                }
            ],
            "js": [
                {
                    "uri": "thirdparty/xlsx/xlsx.full.min.js"
                }
            ]
        }

Personally I don't remember seeing this "ui5://" like you have (maybe is some old way of doing it) but as the file is inside your app you can use just relative paths which will work fine no matter if you deploy your apps in S/4HANA or in BTP.

entry.title: Has anyone Imported Content using a SCORM zip file from Certus? (Paulson molding training)
entry.content:
Certus has a zip file of 14 classes that I should be able to import from just having the file.  Our company was white listed by Certus so the initial error I was getting went away.  After Import Content and editing the ids of all 14 classes, I then made an item to try and launch 1 course.  All it does is show three dots as if it is tryhing to work.

What I would like to know is this:

  1. Should the url go to our orgaization's iContent server?  LinkedIn Learning is integrated.  Their content objects go directly to their server.
  2. If someone has imported a zip file from Certus, are you able to let me know if you are going to your iContent server then over to Certus, then back to your organization.

If you have an instruction sheet of any sort, please share.

Thank you for your help in advance.

Stacey

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA product roadmap vs S/4HANA releases
entry.content: Would be nice if the roadmap eventually gets adjusted to the new release cycles... https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73555000100800004663&range=CURRENT-LAST#Q2%202025

entry.title: Re: Material Consumption Report- Movement types
entry.content: Hi Dominik, thanks for insight. Yes, agree that a Consumption posting indicator must be present for the relevant Movement type. However, I believe still not all Movement types with the indicator are used for Consumption posting alone (Example: Move. type 301 has the indicator present but the material may or may be posted as consumption at the receiving plant). Alternatively, I found table MSEG with the exact consumption quantities for plant and Material combination year wise and I think it would be an appropriate solution in my case. Please let me know otherwise.

entry.title: Re: Integration of SAP Service and Asset Manager & SAP Maintenance Assistant with SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Hi Siva, please use the following external link to access the content. Let me know if you are having issues with the link. 

https://sapvideo.cfapps.eu10-004.hana.ondemand.com/?entry_id=1_hapcp6gs

Thanks

Syam

entry.title: Tips and Tricks – Time Valuations with next day combinations (working or non working day filters)
entry.content:
In the recent 1H 2025 release, the Time product has come up with one of the most awaited features where you can now work with Time Filters which can determine whether next day is a working day Or a non working day.  Gone are those days where we had to write filters with fixed days like Saturdays and Sundays as non working days. This filter now would dynamically detect the non working days based on the work schedule. The non working days can be anyday literally and that’s the beauty of this new enhancement and filter.

I recently came across a  requirement which is represented below and we have made use of this new enhanced Time Records Filters to overcome this challenge. Hence I thought may be I can share practically how we used this new filter to address this.

The requirement is represented in the diagram below:

Requirement

 

The Employee has a planned working time of 08:00 to 17:00 hours which is net 8 hours excluding break.  As you can also see from above, The overtime threshold for working day and  Off Day is different. We need to extract the overtime for current working day and also the next day which is a non working day . And further apply different thresholds for working day and non working day.

In addition, the time between 23:00 to 24:00 in the current working day should be calculated as part of next day between 24:00 to 07:00. In Essence, the non working day overtime calculation for next day also should take into account this 1 hour before the midnight or the cross over. This becomes also a bit tricky

1. In above example the employee has worked a total of 16 hours overtime spanning across midnight until 07:00 AM next day

                For working day the first 1 hour of Overtime is paid at 1.5 , anything above 1 hour is paid at 2.0

2. Similarly for non-working day (remember the non-working day  next day also has now the 1 hour segment from 23:00 to 24:00 from current working day) the thresholds are:

                First 7 hours of Overtime at 2.0 and anything beyond 7 hours gets 3.0

In this case we need different thresholds since a working day is followed by a non working day (next day). But if the case was a working day followed by a working day itself, then we would have the same threshold for overtime across both these days including next day ( where only rule 1 above would have applied)

For more information on the new enhanced filter, you can refer to the blog from my colleague here :

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/finally-it-s-here-now-time-valuation-can-check-if-tomorrow-s-a-working-day/ba-p/14022610

 

Now since the requirement is clear, let’s see how we can work through our time valuations which uses different thresholds for working day followed by a non-working day and how we can leverage the new time records filter enhancement (for next day working/non-working day) to achieve this

 

The below is the sequence of time valuations. (Don’t worry I will paste the screenshots of all time valuations here)

I have covered the case of working day followed by a non working day as part of this example. But similar structure needs to be repeated for other cases like working day followed by a working day or a non working day followed by a non working day or a non working day followed by a working day etc

The First 8 time valuations are pretty standard and straight forward. In these time valuations, we essentially get the scheduled working time without breaks followed by Paid/Recorded times without breaks and then eventually calculate the net overtime .   The below are the screenshots of the same

With the above time valuations you would reach a point where overtime per day is fed into a Time Type Group (TTG) Get OT For Next Steps. You don’t have to necessarily follow the approach I mentioned above but eventually which ever approach you follow, you need the overtime for a day including next day in a time type group to start with. The actual game starts from here now

 

Time valuation to filter overtime before 11 PM of current day

The below time valuation uses the OT For Next Steps as input and filters the overtime for the current working day before 11 PM. Since if you remember in this requirement the time segment from 11 PM to midnight 12 PM is part of next day calculation

Time valuation to filter overtime before between 11 PM to 12 AM (Midnight) and next day 12 AM to 07:00 AM

Similar to above, the next two time valuations filters overtime for CD 11 PM to 12 AM and also next day 12 AM to 07:00 AM. Pretty simple

Time valuation to merge overtime of 11 PM – 12 AM and 12 AM – 07 AM next day into one time type group

 

Since the time segment from 11 PM to 12 AM for current day has to be processed as part of next day 12AM – 07 AM , the results of the above two time valuations are aggregated together and

Stored in a TTG Final Consolidated OT (23:00 to 07:00) . Also considering the fact that different thresholds would apply to Current working day and next day non working day, this segregation via filters is important.

Time valuation to get scheduled working time for planned working days

In the below time valuation, we extract the scheduled working time except holidays and non-working days. This is then filtered out and stored in TTG Scheduled working day for planned days

We would use this in future time valuations as we go along…

Time valuation to check if the current day is a working day and followed by a next day which is a non working day

This is the most critical time valuation now. In this , we essentially check if the current day is a working day and next is a non working day.  We use the result of this time valuation as a flag in next set of time valuations for further differentiating or handling the thresholds for Current Working Day OT and Next day non working day OT separately

This is how the time records filter is defined:

 

As you can see from above, this filters out the Scheduled working day or planned days  where the current day is a planned day and next day is a non working day.

Essentially For the current day, a Time Segment Filter is defined that is linked to a Time Records Filter checking if the next day is a non-working day. If there is a work schedule defined between time segment 12 AM to 12 AM for the entire current day and provided next day is a non working day, the planned day is then moved to TTG below ZTestNK_CWD_ND_NWD_YES . This TTG now can be used as a flag in further  time valuations to carry out the split threshold  Logic between working day followed by a  non-working day.

Now this is the beauty of the new enhanced time recording filter. With a combination of working and non working day (works as AND) in time records filters we could figure this out by just one time valuation

Time valuation to handle the current working day OT

From the time valuation of the previous step (ZTestNK_CWD_ND_NWD_YES) , we filtered out the days where the current day is a working day and is followed by a non working day (next day).

We use this as a comparison group and check if its > 0. If this is true, this means we need to process the current working day overtime separately with a different threshold

Hence move or route the input OT CD Before 11 PM (the Overtime for current day) to TTG Above CWD OT Separate Handling.

Time valuation to handle the next working day OT

From the time valuation of the previous steps (ZTestNK_CWD_ND_NWD_YES) , we filtered out the days where the current day is a working day and is followed by a non working day (next day).

We use this as a comparison group and check if its > 0. If this is true, this means we need to process the next day non working day overtime also separately with a different threshold

Hence move or route the input Final Consolidated OT (11PM to 07 AM) (the Overtime for next non working day) to TTG ND NWD OT Separate Handling

Time Valuations to split the Current Working Day OT and Next Day Non-Working Day OT Separately

 

The Below two time valuations now splits the Overtime for Current Working Day (present in TTG CWD OT Separate Handling) Separately with its own threshold

Also the Overtime for next day non working day (present in TTG ND NWD OT Separate Handling) is split separately with its own threshold

Remember these TTGs are filled only if current day is working day and next day is non working day. If its any other combination like current day is working day and next day is also working day, its not filled at all

This way with a combination of current working day and next day non working day along with other time valuations which use “Compare Threshold with Comparison Group to Route Input” we can handle different OT rules with different thresholds and Its not just for this combination but in principle any other combinations too like Current day is working day and next day is also working day

 

The Last time valuation is optional if you want to aggregate these results together. Hence I will not cover the aggregation part as its pretty simple

TESTING

 

Now let’s test this to see if we get the results as explained in the requirement diagram

The employee Records the working time from 6:00 AM to next day 7:00 AM (I know its too long working hours 😊 but for sake of testing lets have this for now). And this he records on a Friday and crossing over to next day which is a non working day. And this is exactly our use case or combination that we are testing

And you see that the OT splits for CWD (Current working Day) and ND_NWD (Next day non working day) are separately and correctly calculated (Please refer the original requirement diagram at the beginning)

Now If I Change this to a working day and next day working day combination, these TTGs are not filled at all.  We have handled this with a combination of next day working and non-working day filters and other time valuations which does the comparisons.

Now I enhance the logic to also handle the current working day and next day working day combination and this will work

As you can see now, with three new time valuations we have also handled the working day – next day working day combination. The TTGs now are displayed as per this combination and are different from the ones we saw earlier for current working day and next day non working day combination

This is the real beauty of time valuations with this new next day working day/non working day filter. You can write the time valuations with so much flexibility and you can control the results the way you want

Hope this helps in your implementation projects

Best Regards

Neelesh

entry.title: Re: Slow GOS export via CMIS interface (BTP / Document Management)

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2023 Available Now
entry.content: 😴

entry.title: Re: FREE_SELECTIONS_DIALOG with Field names
entry.content:
Thank you for the answer!
I tried to use FIELD_TEXTS filled it with data, but I cant use it in FM FREE_SELECTIONS_DIALOG. There is no parameter for it.

DATA: gs_field_texts TYPE rsdstexts,
			gt_field_texts TYPE TABLE OF rsdstexts.

LOOP AT gt_rowtab INTO gs_rowtab.
	SELECT  dd03l~tabname
					dd03l~fieldname
					dd04t~scrtext_m
		INTO  gs_field_texts
		FROM  dd03l
		INNER JOIN dd04t
			ON  dd03l~rollname    =   dd04t~rollname
			AND dd04t~ddlanguage  =   sy-langu
			AND dd04t~as4local    =   'A'
		WHERE dd03l~tabname     =   gs_rowtab-prim_tab
			AND dd03l~as4local    =   'A'
			AND dd03l~fieldname   <> 'MANDT'.
		APPEND gs_field_texts TO gt_field_texts.
	ENDSELECT.
ENDLOOP.

CALL FUNCTION 'FREE_SELECTIONS_INIT'
	EXPORTING
		kind          = 'T'
		expressions   = gt_free-expr
	IMPORTING
		selection_id  = gv_selid
		where_clauses = gt_free-where
		expressions   = gt_free-expr
		field_ranges  = gt_free-field_r
	TABLES
		tables_tab    = gt_rowtab
		fields_tab    = gt_free-fields
		field_texts   = gt_field_texts.

Pattern for dialog on my system:

CALL FUNCTION 'FREE_SELECTIONS_DIALOG'
  EXPORTING
    selection_id                  = 
*   TITLE                         = ' '
*   FRAME_TEXT                    = ' '
*   STATUS                        =
*   AS_WINDOW                     = ' '
*   START_ROW                     = 2
*   START_COL                     = 2
*   NO_INTERVALS                  = ' '
*   JUST_DISPLAY                  = ' '
*   PFKEY                         =
*   ALV                           = ' '
*   TREE_VISIBLE                  = 'X'
*   DIAG_TEXT_1                   =
*   DIAG_TEXT_2                   =
*   WARNING_TITLE                 =
*   AS_SUBSCREEN                  = ' '
*   NO_FRAME                      =
* IMPORTING
*   WHERE_CLAUSES                 =
*   EXPRESSIONS                   =
*   FIELD_RANGES                  =
*   NUMBER_OF_ACTIVE_FIELDS       =
  TABLES
    fields_tab                    =
*   FCODE_TAB                     =
*   FIELDS_NOT_SELECTED           =
* EXCEPTIONS
*   INTERNAL_ERROR                = 1
*   NO_ACTION                     = 2
*   SELID_NOT_FOUND               = 3
*   ILLEGAL_STATUS                = 4
*   OTHERS                        = 5
          .
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
*         WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: After S4 upgrade ,style.css files are not loading for...
entry.content: I initially tried with "uri": "css/style.css" but it didn't work

entry.title: sap ovp table with dynamic collumns
entry.content:
Hi,

My requirements is to show a collumn with the customer and the others will be the month/year dynamically. Like below:

CUSTOMER | MONTH1/YEAR1 | MONTH2/YEAR1 | .. | MONTH12/YEAR1 | MONTH1/YEAR2 | MONTH2/YEAR2 etc

Is it possble to add dynamic collumns for a sap.ovp.cards.table ? It's mandatory to be in a overview page because I have many others cards working there.

Or, if there are any other suggestions, I'm accepting too.

BR

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
Hi Tax experts, 

 

As per New York zoning Westchester County (NY0F) need to be assigned to Zone 2

Updated table T5UTW as below (Screenshot 1) so tax type 019 is getting generated, but we noticed employees assigned NY0F also got assigned NY02 authority cwhich is incorrect.

Screenshot 1

Screenshot 2

Can you help us achieve NY02 have authority NY0F; and Ny0F not have authority Ny02 assigned so right folks have tax type 019 calculated.

Thankyou

Anjali Nelson

entry.title: Newcomer from the WalkMe Team!
entry.content:
Hi Everyone!

I am the Director of Community Programs for WalkMe! I am so excited to be a part of the SAP Community! If you are interested in joining WalkMe Community events to learn more about WalkMe, reach out to me at community@walkme.com and I'll send you a registration link! WalkMe for SAP SuccessFactors HCM 

WalkMe's signature virtual event, Realize 2025 is coming up on June 17, 2025! Come learn more about how WalkMe brings digital adoption to life through real stories, bold strategies, and standout leaders.  Register here!

~Mary Greening

entry.title: ERROR could not build the "incidentmanagementincidents" module: could not execute the "npm run build
entry.content:
While running tutorial 

Deploy in SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Runtime

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/deploy-to-cf.html

 

at step mbt build ( Assemble with the Cloud MTA Build Tool)
mbt build 

error:

found 0 vulnerabilities
[2025-06-06 17:04:53] INFO executing the "npm run build" command...
npm error Missing script: "build"
npm error
npm error To see a list of scripts, run:
npm error npm run
npm error A complete log of this run can be found in: /home/user/.npm/_logs/2025-06-06T17_04_53_939Z-debug-0.log
[2025-06-06 17:04:53] ERROR could not build the "incidentmanagementincidents" module: could not execute the "npm run build" command: exit status 1
make: *** [Makefile_20250606170437.mta:37: incidentmanagementincidents] Error 1
Error: could not build the MTA project: could not execute the "make -f Makefile_20250606170437.mta p=cf mtar= strict=true mode=" command: exit status 2

entry.title: Re: Global Running Day with SAP Community in Melbourne - Wednesday 4th June 2025
entry.content:
I made it !! I walked/ran 7km !!

Here I started to warming up and walking.

At the middle of the race I stop a bit to capture this beautiful view

and here, almost ended, but I stopped again to take pics lol

Finally 7km ended, and of course, a pic at the finish line

and as prize, I went up the stairs to...

...to sat here and...

... and, enjoy this view alone and meditating about 1 hour, so relaxing and peaceful activity on the nature.

Ang here I share the summary of the run/walk. I got surprised because I had all the year without running, so I think It's a pretty good result after not having raced for quite some time.

entry.title: Re: FREE_SELECTIONS_DIALOG with Field names
entry.content: It's the one in FREE_SELECTIONS_INIT.

entry.title: How to enable buffering for the RAP BO of a customizing table
entry.content:
Introduction

For a scalable and high-performance ABAP application, configuration data should always be accessed using the ABAP application server buffer.

In this blog, you will learn how to provide buffered access to your configuration data for your application and other software components if you have created a RAP BO for the customizing table.

This blog is relevant for

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition  
  • SAP BTP ABAP environment  

Further reading:

  • Related blog posts
  • Learn how you can use ABAP technology to develop innovative applications and business solutions across SAP’s portfolio on SAP Learning Site.

Direct access to the table

In your application code, you can use ABAP SQL to access the customizing table directly.
This is the leanest way to access the data. A valid pattern is to encapsulate the SQL statements in an API class.

To enable buffering, open the technical settings of the table and select buffering state and buffering type. Activate the table.

Unlike the use of ABAP SQL with CDS view or ABAP EML, no access control is performed by default. See ABAP help documentation when choosing the buffering type.

Other applications should not use ABAP SQL statements on your configuration tables. They can use a (released) version of the aforementioned API class but the standard pattern is to release a CDS view.

CDS View

Other applications should use a (released) version of a CDS view to access the configuration data of your application.

Before releasing the CDS view, you should test an example ABAP SQL statement to ensure that the buffer is used.
Create an executable ABAP class with such a statement. In ADT, right-click and select Profile As -> ABAP Application (Console). Continue with the default settings. Then open the ABAP Traces view and then open the recorded trace.
Navigate to the "Database Accesses" section and make sure that 1 is entered in the "Buffered Accesses" column.

  METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.
    SELECT SINGLE * FROM zi_pwid WHERE id = 'TWO' INTO @DATA(result).
  ENDMETHOD.

 Buffer is not used:

Buffer is used:

To enable buffering for the CDS view, you need to create a CDS view entity buffer. For more information, see https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/buffering-cds-view-entities/ba-p/13520422

ABAP EML

It is not recommended to use ABAP EML to read configuration data. If you still want to create a read-only projection RAP BO for your configuration data for other applications, you need to create buffer objects for the CDS view entities for buffered access.

In the SAP BTP ABAP Environment, I could not create a trace where you can see if the buffer is used or not. If you have access to transaction ST05, you can check it.

  METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.
    READ ENTITY zi_pwid
    ALL FIELDS WITH VALUE #( ( id = 'TWO'  ) )
    RESULT DATA(read_result).
  ENDMETHOD.
Buffer is not used:
 
 

 Buffer is used:

entry.title: Re: Why QUnit and sinon versions included in SAPUI5 are s...

It still does feel confusing seeing them in the html, you're right. But at least you have a conclusion for now on how you can/should proceed. I'm thinking about some middleware from SAP right now that should also make testing easier and get rid of a few folders ... let me find it.

Tyler

entry.title: Re: Annotation @AbapCatalog.extensibility.allowNewDatasou...
entry.content:
@junwu , The following extract is taken from your link.

"Extension Data Sources and Compositions An original data source must be explicitly enabled for new associations, new data sources and compositions. If that's not the case, no new data sources or compositions can be introduced to the data model. The annotations @AbapCatalog.extensibility.allowNewDatasources allows you to add new data sources. These data sources need to comply with the respective stability requirements."

Here in my case @AbapCatalog.extensibility.allowNewDatasources is marked as false, then how it allows me get fields from my custom table?

entry.title: Re: Why QUnit and sinon versions included in SAPUI5 are s...
entry.content: Ah my bad, I was thinking about the UI5ers Live where https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sap-ux/preview-middleware was shown with the test FLP setup addition. 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Integration | SAP Concur
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Can we integrate Concur with S/4 Public Cloud using MDI (Master Data Integration) for replicating master data OR is it going to be ICS way?

Thanks!

entry.title: Check the /SCWM/DE_AUNIT_KEEP box when creating warehouse tasks.
entry.content:
Hello experts,

Thank you very much in advance for your collaboration. I have been asked that when a warehouse task is created, the checkbox to fix UMA, data element /SCWM/DE_AUNIT_KEEP, should be automatically marked. However, I have not been able to identify a BADI or EXIT that can help with this request. Does anyone know where I can make this modification?

entry.title: Re: Material Consumption Report- Movement types
entry.content:
Hi @SB1184  Each consumption is a goods issue. However not every goods issue is a consumption. Therefore I don't quite get what you mean by:

Move. type 301 has the indicator present but the material may or may be posted as consumption at the receiving plant

Movement 301 can't post consumption at the receiving plant, as it is a receipt at the receiving plant not an issue. I'm not quite sure which MSEG field you get the consumption from. But if it works for you, use it.

If you think your movement types are not correctly classified in OMJJ as consumption or not, fix those settings first instead of building a custom logic.

entry.title: Re: Material Consumption Report- Movement types
entry.content:
Hi Dominik, for instance, I did a 301 movement from one plant to another and I see material is received as a stock material at the receiving plant, however it is still posted as consumption in the issuing plant (the settings in OMJJ trasc. for 301 movement has "R" indicator for consumption posting). Is this still correct to post as consumption even though the material is received as stock?

In the MVER table, you will find the total consumption for period 6 as 1 which is the result of the 301 movement I did as explained above and this is the data I was planning to obtain for total consumption for each month/year.

entry.title: Re: Ensure License Compliance
entry.content:
Hi,

I did the above steps (added to manifest.json)  but the Consumption report and Show Report does nothing. I can confirm that Show Report is active. I do not see the consumption authority added to the backofficeadministratorrole. I added it myself, logged out and back in but same issue. If I "Reset Everything" then both the Consumption menu and Show Report button are gone!!

We are on 2211.39

Pascal

entry.title: Re: Live Tax E-Reporting in SAP DRC - Digital Reporting R...
entry.content:
Hi BuzJay,

SAP will address the specific ViDA points the closer we get to the compliance date. Please be assured that any country solutions SAP provides will be in compliance with ViDA. We as SAP use DRC as well and need to comply ; )

Best regards,

Martin

entry.title: Re: Calculate Quota Baseline with Standard Calculation Type in SAP Territory and Quota
entry.content: When I am trying to set the Quota Baseline, Historical and Pipeline do not appear as calculation options for me.  Is there something I need to do to enable these ?  I have loaded some historical and opportunity data already.

entry.title: Override Federal Tax Rate for Infotype 267 Payment
entry.content:
Hello All,

We have a group of people who would like their bonus to have a higher federal tax rate (US) than standard. These bonuses are processed from Infotype 267. I am aware of solutions to override taxes during regular payroll processing, such as IT 234, however I have not found a way to do this for an Off-Cycle.

Does anyone have advice on how to do this?

Thanks,
Tyler

entry.title: ICM0 ou IPI0 para IVA de serviço - Inserir impostos para todos os itens
entry.content:
Olá,

Estou passando por um situação em um cliente onde ao lançar faturas para serviços via MIRO, me deparo com o erro 'Inserir impostos para todos os itens' - Nº mensagem 8B674 em ambiente S/4HANA.

Testando e buscando a solução, notei a necessidade de associar ICM0 ou IPI0 aos IVAs para os processos de serviços. Assim a fatura é lançada. Mas me parece estranho ter ICMS e IPI para serviços.

Existe alguma nota SAP que trate disso? Existe outra alternativa? Qual a explicação?

Agradeço desde já.

#LocalizaçãoBrasil #Serviços #S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Exciting Update: SAP SuccessFactors Customer Training Courses are Moving to learning.sap.com!
entry.content:
@romanweise - You are welcome.  I will confirm the timing and let you know when I have an update.

Thanks!
Tamara

entry.title: New E-learning course on S/4HANA TM - core features, deployment options, migration and data volume
entry.content:
The SAP Transportation Management application integrates fleet and logistics management across your network, significantly reducing transportation complexity.

This comprehensive course provides an overview of SAP Transportation Management as part of SAP Digital Supply Chain, detailing deployment options and strategies for data management and archiving.
SAP Transportation Management can be deployed as a standalone solution that integrates with ERP applications like SAP ECC, SAP S/4HANA, and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, or implemented within SAP S/4HANA directly. Organizations transitioning to SAP TM within SAP S/4HANA benefit from improved integration, real-time analytics, and enhanced performance, yet need to carefully plan and execute the migration process.

Learn how to manage large data volumes effectively with Data Volume Management (DVM) and explore archiving options to ensure system optimality and performance continuity.
This course will guide you through deployment strategies and data management tactics to optimize your SAP Transportation Management system.

Goals:

  • Understand SAP Transportation Management core functionalities, benefits, and integration within the SAP ecosystem.
  •  Explore deployment options for SAP TM within SAP S/4HANA, covering both embedded and side-by-side approaches.
  • Gain insights into the compatibility scope between LE-TRA within SAP S/4HANA and SAP TM.
  • Understand strategies in data volume management and explore archiving options to optimize SAP TM performance. 

The customer e-learning has now been published by SAP Enterprise Support Academy. 

Access the course  : SAP Digital Supply Chain – SAP Transportation Management

Disclaimer: Before you can access the link, a one-time registration in the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by-step guidance can be found here.

entry.title: Re: Usage of Data Crawler in Condition using BRF+ approach in SAP TM
entry.content:
Great post @SouravBhaumk!!

I was wondering, what would be the difference between using Data Crawler Access Definition or using Direct BO Data Access Definition? 

Regards

Dragos

entry.title: Re: PO change in SAP
entry.content: Hi CCSRINI9 - SPED is output procedure, where you can configure triggering point for IDOC, when output type trigger it will also generate an outbound IDOC and make the changes in target doc.

entry.title: Re: After S4 upgrade ,style.css files are not loading for SAP ui5 apps in flp
entry.content: how the css reqeust looks like from your dev tool?

entry.title: Re: After S4 upgrade ,style.css files are not loading for SAP ui5 apps in flp
entry.content: I don't think there is any change in this regard.

entry.title: Re: Material Consumption Report- Movement types
entry.content: Hi Dominik, for instance, I did a 301 movement from one plant to another and I see material is received as a stock material at the receiving plant, however it is still posted as consumption in the issuing plant (the settings in OMJJ trasc. for 301 movement has "R" indicator for consumption posting). Is this still correct to post as consumption even though the material is received as stock? In the MVER table, you will find the total consumption for period 6 as 1 which is the result of the 301 movement I did as explained above and this is the data I was planning to obtain for total consumption for each month/year.

entry.title: Re: Visualize os descontos realizados para o Crédito do Trabalhador em nosso novo relatório
entry.content: Oi Joveni, a transação será entregue na próxima entrega. A principio será PC00_M37_EE_INSTDISC.

entry.title: Re: SSO not working. OAuth2UserTokenExchange. Unable to l...
entry.content: @gaurav_setu You don't have to append "?grant_type=client_credentials" to the token service URL. I cloned the auto-created destination, changed the authentication type to OAuth2Password, and set the client secret. A 500 error often indicates an incorrect credentials issue. Please verify the password as well.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Release 2505
entry.content: @adhimassetianeg what do you mean? You can do this already in 2023 release (and probably before that) using a custom entity.
